one_line_ask: Clean up and cut out one raw phone shot of the Dewdrop lip-oil bottle onto a white e-commerce background, then recolor the translucent oil into 8 on-brand shade variants while keeping the metallic collar, cap, and lighting/shadow identical across all of them, and emit a variant spec sheet.
brief: Lumora Beauty Co. is launching "Dewdrop," a glossy translucent lip oil in a frosted-glass dropper bottle with a brushed-gold collar and cap. We only had time to photograph ONE physical sample on an iPhone against a messy studio bench, so we need you to turn that single raw shot into a full, launch-ready shade range for our Shopify PDP and Amazon listing. First, clean up the raw photo: it's slightly tilted, a touch cool/dull, and the background is cluttered. Straighten it, correct the tone and white balance so the glass reads clean and neutral, and crop it square to our 2000x2000 px listing frame. Then cut the bottle out cleanly and place it on a pure white (#FFFFFF) e-commerce background — Amazon-compliant, no drop-shadow baked in, product centered with a little margin. Next, the important part: recolor JUST the translucent oil inside the bottle into 8 shade variants for the range — Clear Gloss (the original, ungraded), Peach Nectar, Rose Quartz, Coral Crush, Berry Tint, Amber Glow, Mauve Mist, and Cherry Sheer. The tint must look like real translucent oil: internal highlights and the light passing through the glass should shift naturally with each color, NOT a flat paint fill. Cri
